message:
fix(guard): align retry-backoff jitter formula with mcp_agent_mail_core::git_cmd::jitter_ms

The pre-commit guard's `guard_run_git_with_retry` used to compute its
per-attempt sleep as:

    jitter = base / 2 + (nanos % base.max(1))

which produces a distribution in [0.5 * base, 1.5 * base) — the retry
could sleep anywhere from half the nominal backoff to one-and-a-half
times it. Meanwhile mcp-agent-mail-core::git_cmd::jitter_ms, which is
the formula the server uses for its own git retries, is explicitly
documented as "deterministic-ish jitter in [0.75x, 1.25x]" and is
implemented as:

    span   = base / 2        // 0.5 * base
    low    = base - span / 2 // 0.75 * base
    offset = nanos % span.max(1)
    jitter = low + offset     // in [0.75x, 1.25x)

The divergence meant the guard's retry cadence was wider and less
predictable than the server's, and in the worst case caused the guard
to sleep up to 2x longer than the server did for the same backoff
attempt. For the three configured backoffs (100, 400, 1600 ms) that's
the difference between [75, 125) / [300, 500) / [1200, 2000) and the
old broader [50, 150) / [200, 600) / [800, 2400).

Guard is now bit-identical to core: same span/low computation, same
modulo, same result range. Added a comment anchoring the guard to
core's definition so future edits keep the two in sync.

No behavior change to the number of retry attempts or to the BACKOFFS_MS
schedule itself — only the jitter distribution applied to each sleep.
